FUNDAMENTALS

Football Analogy

💡 I’m sure that at least once in your life, you watched a football match. The players that run and pass the ball to each other. The desire of a team to score one goal more than the other team. The coach which shouts to the players how they have to be positioned on the pitch.

“Hey Zero, what is this? I thought to be on a crypto lesson, not a course to become a football manager”. Yes, I know, but for how strange it seems, we can learn a lot from football and apply some theories to crypto.

1. Assembling the team

You need to imagine yourself as a coach. Which players do you need to win a game? A mix of goalkeepers, defenders, midfielders and strikers. That's how the game of football works and that's how the same idea can be applied to build our crypto team, aka our portfolio. This is an infographic that I recently made where we have:

  • The goalkeeper (cash)

Cash helps you to prevent portfolio downsides, therefore taking fewer goals from the opponent team.

  • The defenders (Bitcoin and Ethereum)

Given their status as blue chips of the sector, they're solid like a strong and rocky defender. Their main role is to save you from the risk of getting into scams and as an edge against extra volatility. They're your safe boat.

  • The midfielders

The mid-caps (1$-10$billion) act as midfielders. Which is the role of a classic midfielder? Help the defenders to prevent goals but also support the strikers to score with assists or direct goals. They're more solid compared to a small cap.

  • The strikers

Strikers are of course the players who must score goals. We can compare strikers to small caps, where their role is to maximize the performance of our portfolio.

2. Time to get on the pitch

A football match is made of different moments. If a team wants to win the game, it needs to understand when is the time to attack and when is the time to defend.

The same rule can be applied to our portfolio. There is a time when you can move a consistent part of it to mid-caps and small-caps and another time when you just have to defend by being predominantly in Bitcoin and cash.

As you can imagine, the watersheds are respectively the bull market and the bear market. What happens if you decide to field 11 Haaland? Odds are that you’re gonna score goals, but also concede a lot, ending up losing the match. What happens instead if you decide to field 11 Ruben Dias? You'll concede very few goals or probably no one, but you'll also score way less, ending up the match in a draw or a sad victory of 1-0.

That's why it's important to be a wise coach, knowing when and which players to field. Multiple victories will lead you to win the the trophy, in the end.

3. Tactics

Let's see a potential tactic to face both bull and bear periods. Take note: This is my personal strategy but it isn't law. You can/need to adapt your strategy depending on your time horizons, goals, and risk tolerance.

Bear period: 🐻

During a bear period, everything goes down, no matter how good the project is. Therefore I always adjust my portfolio to be 85% in cash | 10% in Bitcoin and 5% in a mid-small cap, just in case it pumps out of nowhere. This happens until I see a trend reversal (Dojo 03 for reference) or a solid risk/reward ratio (-70% on BTC is a steal to me). When I decide it's time to buy, I slowly DCA in Bitcoin out of my cash position. I'm not interested in altcoins, at the start. I'm just keeping my eyes open.

Bull period: 🐂

During the bull period, everything changes and I'm willing to take more risks. My portfolio is adjusted to have 20% BTC | 20% ETH | 3/4 ALTS (Mid/Small) with 20/20/20 or 20/20/10/10. I'm not a fan of “diversification” in altcoins, I prefer to stick to 3/4 projects as I can follow them more closely and concentrate the capital, thus more potential gains to be made.

These are the 2 tactics I adopt for playing the match.

4. Capitalizing on opportunities

I like to think of my assets/coins as players, especially when it's the moment of taking profits. Have you ever seen what happens during the football market season? Depending on the past season's performances, players’ value goes up or down, and there are clubs that are willing to pay astronomical prices to purchase a player.

Do you remember the transfer of Neymar from Barcellona to Paris Saint Germain? It was the largest transfer of all time with a gig + price tag that cost 500 million dollars. This is something that makes me think. What happens if a football club overpays a player that in the next season doesn't respect the past performance? The club will take a loss.

And this is a fantastic concept to use for capitalizing on our assets. Let's suppose that our asset continues to pump and we're in a solid gain. We're now at a watershed: “Should I sell it or should I continue to hold it?” Despite the technical approaches, the mindset plays a key role.

If I’m gonna sell my asset, the odds are that:

  • It continues to pump (the player keeps performing)
  • It starts to dump (the player underperforms)

We tend to make the mistake of thinking that our assets will continue to go up forever. What if they don't? Football clubs capitalize on the request for a player and look to buy a future promise (ALTS), an already strong player (BTC/ETH) or simply wait for the next opportunity. You can do some talent scouting in the meanwhile, so you'll be ready to potentially pick up a new talented player. Think of yourself as a club that capitalizes on players from season to season and looks for new players to strengthen the team and strike for major victories. That's the correct approach to use if you want to actually increase your ways of making it.

5. Targeting the Champions League

Each of us has a “Champions League” goal, a supreme and highest target. For someone can be financial freedom, for another one is a new house or a car, for example. Whatever your goal is, it cannot be achieved in a glimpse of an eye. Do you know why the majority fails in financial markets? Because they have a very short mindset, not because of technical skills.

There are football teams that dream to win the Champions League for years or decades and only with a solid schedule and day-by-day work, they achieve the ambitious trophy. This is a concept that I want you to truly absorb. Why? Because Zero already made the mistake of thinking that “everything should happen quickly” or “it's not enough”. And trust me, is one of the worst mistakes I made because positioned myself in a hurry condition, avoiding rational thinking and making additional mistakes. My best and most honest tip is to make small consistent steps every day and be grateful for what you have achieved.

“Another 5% and I'm out”

“It will go to “x” target, I’m not gonna sell it before”

These are all cursed statements that stop you from truly achieving the final goal because even if those hypothetical targets arrive, you'll want more and more. The only way to win your Champions League is to train daily and appreciate the goals you scored. Your trophy will be far way achievable using this approach and you'll not occur in the risk of destroying years and years of sacrifices.

Conclusion

  • Evaluate the current condition

Is it the time to attack or the time to defend?

  • Field the players

Defensive tactic: BTC | ETH | cash

Offensive tactic: Mid caps | Small caps | Less BTC, ETH

  • Capitalize

Indecent proposal incoming? The player should be sold. What if I hold it? What if it doesn't perform?

  • Look for new opportunities

Got cash I made selling my performing players, which is the next young talent I'm gonna hire?

  • Step-by-step attitude

The season is ending. What are the goals I achieved? Be proud of them.

What are the things that could be improved? Work to fix them.

Remember that this isn't a race, you're not competing with anyone rather than yourself. Be patient, trust the process and make rational moves day by day. That's the secret to winning your Champions League.
